Title: For Debbie : Print orientation error
Notes:
Kyocera Printer Issue – Portrait Printing Problem

The issue with the Kyocera printer involved difficulties when printing documents in portrait orientation. Initially, to troubleshoot, we tested printing using different types of pages and document formats to determine if the problem was related to specific files or paper types. Despite these efforts, the issue persisted.

Next, a diagnostic test page was printed directly from the printer to assess its internal functions and print capabilities. The test page also exhibited the same portrait printing problem, indicating that the issue was not related to the document or driver settings but more likely tied to the printer's software or drivers.

To resolve this, the existing printer drivers were completely removed from the system to eliminate any potential corruption or configuration errors. Afterward, the latest Kyocera drivers were downloaded from the official website and installed freshly on the device. Following the reinstallation, test prints were conducted in portrait mode, and the problem was successfully resolved.

This step-by-step approach ensured that the root cause was identified and addressed, restoring full printing functionality in portrait orientation.

Title: Re: Video display issue
Notes:
David reported issue for fabian  that when attempting to screen-share in Google Meet, the shared content displayed severe color distortion, including pink/green overlays, banding, and duplicated artifacts. The issue occurred only during screen sharing and not during normal display usage.

Troubleshooting Performed

  1. Investigated common causes of Google Meet display distortion:

    • Hardware acceleration glitches in Chrome/Edge

    • Outdated or corrupted GPU drivers

    • Screen capture permission issues (macOS)

    • Conflicts with virtual camera/background software

    • High-resolution display capture issues

  2. Focused on Hardware Acceleration (most common cause).

    • Verified user was using Google Chrome.

    • Disabled Hardware Acceleration:

      • Chrome → Settings → System → Use hardware acceleration when available → Off

    • Restarted Chrome.

  3. Confirmed system/browser fully updated.

    • Performed Chrome update to latest version.

    • Relaunched browser.

Resolution

Disabling Hardware Acceleration in Google Chrome and updating the browser resolved the screen-share distortion issue immediately. User confirmed the Google Meet share now displays normally with no color artifacts.

Title: Dynamic Group Creation & Huntress Learner Sync Configuration for AOC
Notes: Configured a new Microsoft Entra ID Dynamic User Group specifically designed to include only licensed mailbox-enabled users. Created the group using a validated dynamic membership rule that filters for active, member-type accounts with an enabled Exchange Online service plan, ensuring only real, licensed users are included. After successfully creating and verifying the dynamic group membership logic, proceeded to integrate Huntress SAT with Microsoft Graph. In the Huntress portal, navigated to Integrations &rarr; Providers, selected the AOC tenant, and chose the newly created dynamic user group (AOC_Users) as the group to sync learners from. Enabled the option to exclude unlicensed and non-human learner identities, ensuring only properly licensed human users can be provisioned as Huntress learners. Confirmed that Use UPN as unique email address was selected to maintain identity accuracy. Activated Automatic Daily Sync so learner accounts are continuously updated based on group membership changes. Also enabled attribute sharing options (Status, First Name, Last Name, Employee ID, Department, Manager) and verified advanced settings related to learner activation/inactivation and department creation. Completed validation of the sync configuration to ensure that only authorised, licensed users will populate within Huntress SAT moving forward

Title: Re: Huntress Low Incident | LOW - Incident on AAOC-28 (AOC - Apartment Association of Orange County)
Notes:
We received a security alert indicating that an unencrypted password file exists on system AAOC-28 at the following path:
C:\Users\cordero.AAOC\Apartment Association of Orange County\Secure - Documents\_ACCOUNTING\_PASSWORDS\Accounting Passwords 12-18-2023.xlsx.

This file contains passwords and is stored in an unencrypted state, posing a significant security risk. Such files are vulnerable to unauthorized access.

Actions Taken:

  1. The file path was confirmed and flagged as a security concern.

  2. We advised Cordero to take one of the following actions:

    • Encrypt the file with a strong password via File > Protect Using Password or Tools > Protect > Encrypt > Encrypt with Password.

    • If the file is no longer needed, delete it to prevent potential security risks.

Next Steps:

  • Follow-up with Cordero to ensure encryption is applied, or confirm deletion of the file.

Status: Awaiting confirmation from Cordero on encryption or file deletion.
